primary_cases: Bethlehem Steel (looper program, Homer Research Labs)
recurring_figures: 1990 steel productivity: one person-hour per ton (integrated); 0.8 person-hours per ton (Chaparral)
scrap steel: ~$100/ton; cast iron from blast furnace: $180/ton
integrated steel mill capital cost: $20 billion; mini mill: 20× less
$30/ton Japanese shipping cost across Pacific (Chaparral's labor-cost benchmark)
Boeing door estimation: 750 doors / 250 employees → $50,000/door (1990s); ~$100,000/door today
75% energy savings from inline reheating + net-shape casting at Chaparral
36 ksi vs 50 ksi structural steel; Chaparral produced one product meeting both specs
reinforced concrete: 4.2 billion tons/year; concrete tensile ~3,000 psi vs steel 30,000–60,000 psi
Willow Glass: 100–200 microns thick (4 or 8 thousandths of an inch)
glass strength: 14,000 psi initial, dropping to ~1,000 psi over a month due to moisture attack
